why_now: Reported vulnerability in widely used MIT krb5 library.
title: CVE-2026-11850: MIT krb5 Integer Underflow
summary: [P1] An integer underflow vulnerability was found in MIT krb5, which could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code. No patch is currently available, and exploitation status is unknown. Why now: Reported vulnerability in widely used MIT krb5 library. (confidence: 0.80)
classification_reasoning: Integer underflow vulnerability allows arbitrary code execution, affecting the foundation layer.

why_now: Reported vulnerability in Cerebrate, a widely used web framework.
title: CVE-2026-53911: Cerebrate Primary Key Vulnerability
summary: [P2] A vulnerability in Cerebrate allows the id primary key field to be supplied, which could allow an attacker to access sensitive data. No patch is currently available, and exploitation status is unknown. Why now: Reported vulnerability in Cerebrate, a widely used web framework. (confidence: 0.70)
classification_reasoning: Vulnerability allows access to sensitive data, affecting the application layer.
